evals.prompt: I'm using enums in TypeScript for user roles. Is this the right approach?
evals.expected_output: Avoid TypeScript enums — they have runtime cost and cause issues with tree-shaking. Use literal unions or as const instead: type Role = 'admin' | 'user' | 'guest' or const ROLES = { ADMIN: 'admin' } as const; type Role = typeof ROLES[keyof typeof ROLES]. This is zero-runtime and fully type-safe.

evals.prompt: How do I make TypeScript enforce that I handle all cases in a union type switch?
evals.expected_output: Use discriminated unions with a literal discriminant field (e.g. kind or type). Add a never exhaustiveness check in the default case: default: { const _exhaustive: never = x; throw new Error('Unhandled case'); }. TypeScript will report a type error if you add a new union member without handling it.

evals.prompt: How do I prevent a string user ID from being accidentally passed where a post ID is expected in TypeScript?
evals.expected_output: Use Branded Types: type UserId = string & { __brand: 'UserId' }. Create a type guard or cast function: function asUserId(id: string): UserId { return id as UserId; }. This prevents mixing up IDs of different types at compile time with zero runtime overhead.

should_not_trigger: How do I set up async/await error handling in TypeScript with custom error classes?
Configure a Go service with Wire dependency injection
Write a pytest fixture for an async SQLAlchemy session
